result.summary: Aqua exposes two documented SaaS HTTP APIs in its developer center (Platform/CSPM and Image Scanning, with auth only partially documented), plus the public Trivy CLI and a public Trivy MCP server plugin.
result.description: Aqua Security provides cloud native security products for containers, Kubernetes, cloud posture management, and software supply chain security. It also maintains Trivy, an open source security scanner and related developer tooling.

aqua_cspm_api_key.label: Aqua CSPM API key pair (access key and secret key)
aqua_cspm_api_key.setup: In the Aqua platform, go to the [Aqua Developer Center](https://cloud.aquasec.com/developers), then open the platform area and create credentials from the [Keys page](https://cloud.aquasec.com/keys). The sample repo linked from the developer center mentions creating and connecting a key using an access key and secret key, and the CSPM API docs include a Signature Creation section, indicating requests are signed with this key pair.
